_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L4 'Basic Scale Mail' keeper and apply the L4→L5 delta to produce the Lvl 5 'Mid Scale Mail' variant (Tier 2 mid). Major silhouette change: hood retires.
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L4/composite/archer_L4_v<keeper>_composited.png (v3 chain, user picks keeper at runtime)
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Archer/Archer_Progression_Plan.md (L5 — Mid Scale Mail; revised 2026-05-07 per AD FeedbackV0)
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chain (single-input chained, locked 2026-05-07) — previous keeper composite is the sole input. No L1_Base scale anchor.
history: v1-v8 (2026-05-04 / 05): see prior history — coif at neck + studded pauldrons + brass bow tips. Hood up throughout. Deprecated by AD FeedbackV0 head-armor reschedule.
v_no_stubble (2026-05-05): Stripped 'faint stubble shadow' from face descriptions. Canonical face is now clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin with no facial hair.

v9_detail_economy (2026-05-07): MAJOR REWRITE per AD FeedbackV0 (CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23): head-armor schedule pulled earlier. (a) HOOD RETIRES at L5 (was L7 in old plan) — drops to shoulders as a red mantle, the cloth hood is no longer up. (b) STEEL SKULLCAP from L4 is now FULLY REVEALED on the top of the head (was a brow rim only inside the hood opening at L4). (c) SCALE-MAIL COIF moves from neck-level (old plan) to FRAME the BACK and SIDES of the head below the skullcap — covers where the hood used to drape; face/throat NOT covered. (d) Brass tip caps on bow retained. (e) STUDDED PAULDRONS DROPPED per detail-economy rule (surface-decoration beat; the head transformation is the L5 silhouette change). (f) PRESERVE block: dropped both hip pouches + 'knife behind right-hip pouch' (L4 v3 has no pouches). (g) Updated paths to v3 chain. Adds count 3 → 4 (hood-retire / skullcap-reveal / coif-back-sides count as 3 tightly-coupled head beats; bow tip caps = 4).

v3_single_input (2026-05-07): Dropped L1_Base from image_urls per locked single-input chained rule (CLAUDE.md Part 1 §B revised). Stripped 'SECOND input image' / scale-anchor language from prompt body and FRAMING block heading. Sole input is the previous composited keeper. urls_before=2, urls_after=1, body_chars_removed=83.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L4/composite/archer_L4_v<keeper>_composited.png>> (CONTENT REFERENCE — the v3 L4 keeper to upgrade)
prompt: CRITICAL — TWO HARD RULES BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE. (A) LIVERY PRESERVATION: the mustard-yellow tunic with the quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels MUST REMAIN THE DOMINANT CHEST GARMENT, FULLY VISIBLE FROM COLLAR TO UPPER HIP, exactly as in the input image. In the input image the scale mail at the body is a SHORT BAND (3-4 inches tall) visible ONLY BELOW the tunic hem. This edit MUST NOT extend or modify that scale-mail band — keep the mustard-yellow tunic, the belt position, and the scale-mail band exactly as in the input image. The mustard-yellow tunic must remain visible BOTH above AND below the belt, ending at the upper-hip tunic hem just as in the input image. DO NOT extend the scale-mail band UPWARD past the tunic hem; DO NOT extend it DOWNWARD into a longer skirt; DO NOT cover the tunic chest with any new garment, mail, jerkin, or vest. The tunic + scale-mail band geometry from the input image is OFF-LIMITS for this edit. (B) SHORT-SLEEVE PRESERVE: the scale-mail sleeves in the input image are SHORT — they cover ONLY the deltoid and upper bicep, ending at mid-bicep in a clean horizontal scalloped edge, leaving a clear band of BARE peachy-tan SKIN visible between the scale-mail short sleeve at mid-bicep and the leather wrist bracer at mid-forearm. This edit MUST NOT extend the scale-mail sleeves further down the arm. The bare-skin bicep + elbow + upper-forearm band must remain exactly as in the input image. NOW APPLY THE FOUR UPGRADES BELOW WITHOUT VIOLATING RULES A OR B. EDIT GOAL — Apply FOUR additive upgrades to the archer in the input image (the 'Basic Scale Mail' content reference) to produce the 'Mid Scale Mail' variant. The signature L5 silhouette change is the HOOD RETIRING and the STEEL SKULLCAP from the previous level being fully revealed on top of the head. KEEP EVERYTHING FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY AS-IS unless explicitly changed by the four upgrades below. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT ===== (1) HOOD RETIRES — DROPS TO SHOULDERS AS A RED CLOTH MANTLE — the deep crimson-red pointed cloth hood that was pulled fully UP over the head in the input image is now FULLY DOWN, draped across the shoulders and the back of the neck as a RED CLOTH MANTLE / SHORT COWL. The mantle is the same deep crimson-red cloth as the original hood (and matches the cape), forming a soft layered cowl that sits at the neck/shoulder line and drapes a few inches across the upper back. The mantle is SEPARATE from the cape — the cape is still attached at both shoulders and falls to knee length on the back; the new red mantle is a shorter additional cloth layer at the shoulders/neck, visible in front of the cape attachment as a soft red drape. THE TOP OF THE HEAD IS NOW UNCOVERED BY CLOTH — the cloth hood is no longer up, no longer pointed, no longer framing the face. The character's face and hair and the steel headpiece are now FULLY VISIBLE. (2) STEEL SKULLCAP FULLY REVEALED ON TOP OF THE HEAD — the steel skullcap that was visible only as a brow rim inside the hood opening at the previous level is NOW FULLY VISIBLE on the top of the head. It is a polished cool silver-gray steel bowl shape, simple and clean, sitting snug on the head from the brow line up over the crown, with TWO small painted iron rivets at the temples (carried over from the previous level) and a soft top-down highlight on the dome of the cap reading as cool silver-gray polished steel. The skullcap is the dominant head feature now that the hood is down. The character's dark-brown short hair is visible at the temples and at the nape of the neck below the skullcap. The skullcap is a SIMPLE BOWL — NOT a tall conical helm, NOT an open-face plate helm with cheek guards, NOT a closed-visor helm; just a low rounded steel cap covering the crown of the head. (3) SCALE-MAIL COIF FRAMES THE BACK AND SIDES OF THE HEAD — add a chunky overlapping silver-gray scale-mail coif (same chunky scale style as the existing short-sleeve scale mail and the body band) that wraps the BACK and SIDES of the head BELOW the steel skullcap, covering the area where the hood used to drape. The coif sits between the lower edge of the skullcap and the new red mantle at the shoulders, visible from the front-three-quarter angle as a silver-gray scale framing the temples + behind the ears + at the nape of the neck. The face / jaw / throat / front of the neck are NOT covered by the coif — only the back and sides of the head, integrating with the steel skullcap above and the red mantle below. (4) BOW UPGRADE — ADD BRASS TIP CAPS AT BOTH LIMB TIPS — add small polished brass sleeves wrapping the OUTERMOST half-inch of each bow limb tip (BOTH the upper limb tip and the lower limb tip). Each cap is a small metallic cone-shape sitting flush at the tip end with a clean buttery-brass highlight on the upper edge. The brass grip-end binding rings from the previous level stay. The bow LIMB material between the grip-end bindings and the new tip caps remains warm wood-brown. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== Every other element of the input image must remain identical: face (chiseled chibi jawline + clean-shaven smooth peachy-tan skin + closed-mouth half-smirk + asymmetric brow with anatomical-LEFT brow higher + warm brown eyes with single catchlight + small upturned nose); the matching crimson-red cape attached at both shoulders falling to knee length with soft folds and inward curl on viewer-LEFT (still present, with the new red mantle layer sitting in front of the cape attachment at the shoulders); both forearms bracered (bow arm single cross-strap, draw arm two cross-strap bindings); both hands BARE peachy-tan; bare anatomical-LEFT hand gripping the recurve short bow vertically at hip height; the recurve short bow with warm wood-brown limbs + dark-brown leather grip wrap + brass grip-end binding rings + pale cream bowstring (now also with the new brass tip caps); brown leather cylindrical back-quiver over anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind viewer-RIGHT side of head with three to four white-feather red-tip arrows; sleeveless mustard-yellow tunic with quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels (visibly dominant on the chest); leather X-strap chest harness diagonally across front from anatomical-LEFT shoulder to anatomical-RIGHT hip with brass ring at crossing; wider single warm-brown leather belt with brass rectangular buckle and visible edge stitching; dark-wood dagger in leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip with brass capstone on handle (STANDALONE — no hip pouches, no satchels, no tool loops accompany it); leather pauldron caps over each deltoid (slightly darker brown than the bracers, single seam over each cap, plain leather — no studs); brown leather greaves on lower legs from boot cuff to just below knee with single cross-strap binding each; olive forest-green knee-length trousers (visible above the greaves); chunky warm-brown leather boots with darker folded ankle cuff; SHORT-SLEEVE scale mail on both shoulders (chunky overlapping silver-gray scales covering ONLY the deltoid and upper bicep, ending mid-bicep in a clean horizontal scalloped edge — these stay exactly as in the input image, do NOT extend the sleeves further down the arm); BARE-SKIN BAND of muscular bicep + elbow + upper forearm visible between the short scale-mail sleeve at mid-bicep and the leather wrist bracer at mid-forearm; SHORT BAND of scale mail (3-4 inches tall) visible at the body BELOW the lower edge of the tunic hem, sitting OVER the trousers and ABOVE the leather greaves. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately 15-25 degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) hangs DOWN at his side with elbow only slightly bent, bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height in front of the hip. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) bent at roughly 90 degrees, FOREARM HORIZONTAL ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest level, bracered hand at chest CENTER in TIGHT CLOSED FIST with KNUCKLES OUTWARD. Calm balanced HERO STANCE; NOT an active draw. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character at ~75% vertical fill (~765px tall), ~57% horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of head (the steel skullcap dome) at ~13.5% from top of canvas. BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas. Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadow recesses, slight thinning under the key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven, never hard black. Clean white background, no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT keep the cloth hood pulled UP over the head — at this level the hood is FULLY DOWN, dropped to the shoulders as a red mantle. DO NOT render the cloth hood as a partial half-up half-down state — it is DOWN at the shoulders. DO NOT render the steel skullcap as hidden under cloth — it is FULLY VISIBLE on top of the head. DO NOT replace the steel skullcap with a tall conical helm, an open-face plate helm with cheek guards, a closed-visor helm, or any other headpiece — at this level the head wears a simple polished steel SKULLCAP only (low rounded bowl shape covering the crown). DO NOT cover the face / jaw / throat / front of the neck with the scale-mail coif — only the back and sides of the head + nape of neck. DO NOT add brass studs to the leather pauldrons — pauldrons stay PLAIN leather. DO NOT cover the mustard-yellow tunic chest panel with scale mail; DO NOT replace the tunic with a scale-mail body coat; DO NOT extend the scale mail UPWARD past the tunic hem; DO NOT shrink the tunic into a thin strip or a short surcoat panel — the tunic must remain the DOMINANT chest garment, fully visible from collar to upper hip with the quilted red center panel and quilted red side panels and mustard-yellow base all clearly visible (the LIVERY PRESERVATION rule); DO NOT extend the scale-mail band at the body into a longer skirt; DO NOT add any new scale-mail skirt or scale-mail extension to the lower body — the previous level's short scale-mail band stays exactly as-is, with the mustard tunic clearly visible BOTH above AND below the belt; DO NOT extend the short scale-mail SLEEVES past mid-bicep — the bare-skin bicep + elbow + upper-forearm band must remain visible; DO NOT add a crossbody arrow bandolier or any second crossbody strap — only the existing X-strap remains across the front of the chest; DO NOT add a leather over-jerkin or chest vest; DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, gold buckle, gold limb tips, or any GOLD accents — only BRASS at this step; DO NOT add steel pauldrons, steel breastplate, steel vambraces, or any plate armor; DO NOT add tassets or arming-skirt strips at the hip; DO NOT add ANY hip pouch, belt pouch, leather satchel, tool-loop ring, or hip-mounted accessory — the dagger sheath is the ONLY belt accessory at this level; DO NOT add a closed visor; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, or engraved patterns; DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow; DO NOT remove or modify the brass grip-end binding rings on the bow; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change any facial feature, hair, or skin tone; DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape; DO NOT change the bow shape, position, or recurve profile; DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips); DO NOT add or remove arrows in the back-quiver; DO NOT change the back-quiver position; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background or add scenery; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
